title: Nexus Profile
description: Schema for Nexus OS Environment Manifest. Per the Protocol: no 'run:' commands are ever allowed.

name.description: Profile identifier, lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens

extends.description: Profile to inherit from — packages are additive, env vars are overridden by child

items.description: Package name — must be a valid package identifier, no shell metacharacters

suggested_distro.description: V16: The Linux distro this profile is designed for. 'any' means it works on all distros. When the current distro differs, the engine warns the user and suggests a Distrobox container or WSL2 reimport.

wsl_available.description: V16: Whether the suggested_distro is available as a WSL2 image. true = user can `nexus wsl import` to switch distro. false = must use Distrobox fallback.

dotfiles.description: Per V7: declarative dotfile management via Chezmoi. Optional; profiles without this section are unaffected.

source.description: HTTPS URL of the Git repository to bind to Chezmoi (e.g., https://github.com/user/dotfiles). Host must be in the dotfiles AllowedSourceHosts whitelist.

apply_on_init.description: When true, 'nexus init' will run 'nexus dotfiles apply' after binding the source. When false, dotfiles are only bound — applied on explicit 'nexus dotfiles apply'.

sync_on_apply.description: V8: When true, 'nexus profile apply' triggers a full pull + apply + push sync after the bind step. Lets profile switching be a sync point. Default false (backward-compatible with V7).

managed_paths.description: Files to track with 'chezmoi add' after the source is bound. Each entry must be an absolute path under the user's $HOME.

sensitive_paths.description: Paths within managed_paths that are sensitive (SSH keys, credentials). Listed for documentation; the engine still requires --force to manage these at runtime.
